briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 5, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Warnings and Realities from the Front Lines of the Climate Crisis
sections.content: Recent research has revealed a shocking finding: existing sea-level rise predictions have been underestimated by at least 1 meter, potentially increasing flood damage by up to 37% in some developing regions [91]. These climate changes are pushing tropical insects to their thermal limits, causing walruses to migrate northward, and even altering the timing of natural flowering, thereby disrupting the overall rhythm of ecosystems [98, 102, 105]. Domestically, direct damages such as reduced electric vehicle battery life due to heatwaves have been reported [8], and daily life is severely impacted, with 9 out of 10 Gwangju citizens suffering from summer heat [14]. Furthermore, warnings have emerged that dangerous microbial groups, such as 'brain-eating amoebas' residing in water and soil, could spread with climate change [104]. News of the crisis in the Alps region and the threatened livelihoods of Greenlandic fishermen clearly illustrates the reality of the climate crisis facing the entire world [96, 97].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Efforts and Challenges Towards Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts towards carbon neutrality are continuing, centered around corporations and local governments. Honda set an example for eco-friendly management by achieving the highest 'A rating' for three consecutive years in the CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) climate change response assessment [2, 4, 5, 9, 11]. The Standard Meteorological Observatory in Gochang, Jeonbuk, has been registered with the WMO (World Meteorological Organization) Global Surface Reference Network, and will play a crucial role in global climate change analysis [1, 6, 15]. Gwangmyeong City and Cheonan City have recruited clubs and signed agreements to spread a citizen-led culture of carbon neutrality practices [23, 24, 25, 29, 30, 34, 40, 42, 57, 61]. Furthermore, various local governments such as Geoje, Gunsan, and Jeju are encouraging citizen participation by operating the 'Carbon Neutrality Point System,' which offers benefits of up to 100,000 KRW for reducing car mileage or saving electricity and water [26, 31, 33, 38, 45, 47, 51, 52, 56, 58, 60, 63, 64]. However, the industry faces an emergency situation due to government pressure to raise the 2030 carbon neutrality target [21]. Discrepancies between policy and reality also exist, such as the omission of international offset mechanisms in carbon neutrality plans for the aviation sector [28], and a lack of interest in supporting carbon-neutral companies on the ground [35]. Additionally, cases of greenwashing, where chemical-based artificial leather is disguised as 'eco-friendly,' have been detected [94], indicating the need for monitoring and efforts for genuine carbon neutrality practices.

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Transition: Opportunities and Obstacles
sections.content: President Lee emphasized that the situation in the Middle East should be seized as an opportunity for a massive transition to renewable energy, urging a swift shift [65, 70, 72]. In line with this, the Jeonnam Regional Headquarters of Korea Rural Community Corporation is expanding the designation of renewable energy zones [67, 76, 84]. Naju City is also promoting 'sunshine pensions' through resident-participatory renewable energy generation projects, building a model that shares profits with citizens [68, 77, 78, 81, 89]. With the full-scale implementation of the quasi-centralized renewable energy system, 470MW of resources are entering the electricity market, creating a new landscape [66, 75]. The industrial ecosystem is also expanding with technological advancements, as K-solar and space solar power emerge as strategic national industries by 2035 [83, 90]. However, renewable energy power plants are at risk of becoming 'stranded assets' due to delays in grid connection [74], and the situation where domestic renewable energy development is hampered by local NIMBYism [87] is a challenge that needs urgent resolution. Furthermore, external environmental uncertainties, such as the decline in performance of ESG and renewable energy investment products due to geopolitical factors like the US-Iran conflict [85], are also acting as obstacles to the renewable energy transition.

sections.title: 🌊 Climate Change Adaptation and the Search for New Solutions
sections.content: In the face of the climate crisis, adaptation efforts to minimize damage and ensure a sustainable future are also active. Wando County Mayor Shin Woo-cheol mentioned the necessity of a major transformation in the fisheries industry amidst the climate crisis, and Wando is exploring solutions for seaweed and abalone farming to cope with climate change [3, 10, 13]. Chungcheongnam-do has registered two patents related to land-based laver farming, leading the development of climate change response technologies and creating a new breakthrough for the laver industry [12, 17]. In positive news, a survey by the Ministry of Oceans and Fisheries last year showed that the proportion of coastal erosion concern/severe areas in Korea decreased by 20.9 percentage points compared to the previous year [92]. In Virginia, a climate change education bill has shown further progress [99], and Boise City has launched a community climate action guide [101], demonstrating ongoing efforts to strengthen resilience through education and community participation. Cultural efforts to raise public awareness of climate change are also noteworthy, such as actor Michael Sheen narrating a new climate change film [95, 108].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing once again acknowledged the severity of the global climate crisis, highlighting various domestic and international response efforts alongside the challenges that need to be addressed. This is a moment when everyone's attention and action are required for a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
